name: BBN expansion and dark radiation from hidden sector
observable: Delta N_eff during BBN; hidden-sector temperature ratio; abundances and lifetimes
missing_observables: dark-sector temperature ratio; decoupling temperature; species abundances; decay widths/lifetimes
applicability: Relevant because the FOPT occurs around T~0.04-0.14 GeV and particles are MeV-GeV scale, but no thermal-history module maps the zero-portal hidden sector to BBN observables.
tested_subregion: v_D=0.652-1.395 GeV; m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; m_chi~0.034-0.108 GeV where beta_lambda>0
source_title: BBN constraints on MeV-scale dark sectors. Part I. Sterile decays

name: BBN electromagnetic entropy injection and photodisintegration
observable: electromagnetic decay lifetime; EM branching ratio; injected abundance
missing_observables: none for exact-zero portal; would need decay widths if portal opened
applicability: Published EM-decay bounds assume decays into photons/electrons or other SM electromagnetic final states. With exact epsilon=0 and lambda_HP=0 in the backend, no SM EM decay channel is specified.
tested_subregion: same preferred mass region; no SM EM coupling
source_title: BBN constraints on MeV-scale dark sectors. Part II. Electromagnetic decays

name: CMB Delta N_eff / extra relativistic energy density
observable: Delta N_eff at recombination; temperature ratio; relativistic degrees of freedom
available_observables: model particle content; preferred parameter region
missing_observables: dark-sector temperature ratio at neutrino decoupling/recombination; stable relativistic content
applicability: Planck-type Neff constraints are directly relevant only after computing the hidden-sector radiation density. The current handoffs do not provide this observable.
tested_subregion: PTArcade 1sigma region; no computed Delta N_eff
source_title: Cosmological constraints in extended parameter space from the Planck 2018 Legacy release

name: CMB energy injection from annihilation/decay
observable: p_ann or decay energy injection into SM final states
available_observables: masses and couplings internal to dark sector; epsilon=0; lambda_HP=0
missing_observables: relic abundance; annihilation cross sections; branching ratios to SM; Sommerfeld enhancement if applicable
applicability: CMB energy-injection bounds on light-mediator dark matter require stable DM abundance and SM-visible final states. Exact-zero portals make direct application unavailable; dark-only annihilation would need a model-specific recast.
tested_subregion: m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; mediator masses from preferred region
source_title: Strong constraints on self-interacting dark matter with light mediators

name: Thermalization and decoupling between dark and visible sectors
observable: Gamma/H for portal interactions; decoupling temperature; initial temperature ratio
missing_observables: initial condition for dark-sector temperature; gravitational or unspecified reheating coupling; portal rate if nonzero
applicability: With zero portal couplings the backend does not thermalize the dark and SM sectors by construction, but the FOPT calculation implicitly uses a dark-sector temperature. Cosmological constraints need the temperature-ratio history.
source_title: US Cosmic Visions: New Ideas in Dark Matter 2017: Community Report

name: Dark photon visible searches in MeV-GeV range
observable: kinetic mixing epsilon; visible decay branching ratios; production cross section
missing_observables: none for exact-zero epsilon; recast if epsilon is relaxed
applicability: Beam-dump, fixed-target, flavor and collider dark-photon limits in this mass range assume nonzero kinetic mixing with SM electromagnetic/hypercharge current. The validated backend fixes epsilon exactly zero.
tested_subregion: m_X overlaps MeV-GeV dark photon searches, but epsilon=0

name: Dark photon invisible / dark matter mediator searches
observable: kinetic mixing epsilon; invisible branching ratio; production cross section
missing_observables: none for exact-zero epsilon; recast if epsilon opened
applicability: Invisible mediator searches still require SM production through kinetic mixing or another visible portal. Exact epsilon=0 removes the production assumption.
tested_subregion: m_X=0.340-0.878 GeV; possible X->psi psi only in subregions if kinematically open

name: Dark scalar Higgs-portal laboratory constraints
observable: Higgs portal lambda_HP; scalar mixing angle; visible decay widths
missing_observables: none for exact-zero portal; scalar decay widths if portal opened
applicability: Higgs exotic decays, beam dumps and direct-detection scalar-mediated bounds require Higgs/scalar mixing or another SM portal. Backend fixes lambda_HP exactly zero.
source_title: Minimal self-interacting dark matter models with light mediator

name: Dark fermion relic abundance
observable: Omega_psi h^2; annihilation cross sections; stability symmetry
missing_observables: stability assignment; Boltzmann calculation; annihilation channels; dark-sector temperature ratio
applicability: The handoffs do not establish whether the degenerate Majorana pair is stable or constitutes all/part of dark matter. Relic constraints cannot be applied from mass alone.
tested_subregion: m_psi=0.055-0.695 GeV; y_D MAP caveat at boundary
source_title: Minimal self-interacting dark matter models with light mediator

name: Dark fermion self-interactions and structure formation
observable: sigma_T/m versus velocity; halo abundance; mediator spectrum
available_observables: masses and dark couplings in preferred region
missing_observables: transfer/viscosity cross sections; relic fraction; velocity-dependent averaging; stable DM assumption
applicability: Light mediator self-interactions may be relevant, but applying Bullet Cluster/halo bounds needs the DM identity, abundance fraction and velocity-dependent cross section.
tested_subregion: m_psi~0.055-0.695 GeV; m_X~0.340-0.878 GeV; m_chi sub-0.11 GeV where physical
source_title: Minimal self-interacting dark matter models with light mediator

name: Percolation and completion under strong supercooling
observable: percolation completion criterion; false-vacuum fraction; reheating temperature; alpha validity
available_observables: FOPT handoff and PTA benchmarks include alpha beta_H Treh; Gaussian percolation approximation
missing_observables: full completion diagnostic beyond backend approximation; wall velocity consistency; vacuum-domination check
applicability: Directly relevant because benchmark 14/14 points include very large alpha. Existing backend has an approximate percolation treatment, so this is flagged as approximate rather than an exclusion.
tested_subregion: benchmark alpha up to ~1.35e12; PTArcade MAP/1sigma not separately audited for completion
source_title: Supercool subtleties of cosmological phase transitions

name: Perturbativity, beta_lambda positivity and Coleman-Weinberg validity
observable: g_D, y_D, beta_lambda, loop expansion, RG/Landau-pole behavior
available_observables: PTArcade 1sigma ranges; beta_lambda formula; no RG module
missing_observables: RG running; Landau-pole scale; CW scale-setting uncertainty; physical-region mask for beta_lambda>0
applicability: Couplings are perturbative in the 1sigma region, but the rectangular posterior includes beta_lambda<0 corners; physical interpretation requires enforcing beta_lambda>0 and RG stability.
tested_subregion: g_D=0.260-0.315; y_D=0.119-0.705; beta_lambda corner range -3.29e-4 to 5.97e-3
source_title: Classically conformal B-L extended Standard Model and Higgs vacuum stability
